Details: Comparison of two Informatica mapping XML outputs for MEMBERSDATA table data quality implementation. First output provides basic source definition structure while second output delivers comprehensive DQ solution with complete transformation pipeline, validation rules, and dual-target architecture for valid/rejected records.

Both outputs address MEMBERSDATA table processing but with vastly different scopes. First output (lines 1-14) provides only source definition with basic field mapping, while second output (lines 1-318) implements comprehensive data quality framework with 14 specific DQ rules, transformation pipeline, and complete ETL workflow. Semantic alignment limited to shared table structure and field definitions.

Fundamental structural differences: First output uses simple MAPPING element with single SOURCE definition, while second output employs full PowerMart repository structure with REPOSITORY>FOLDER>SOURCE/TARGET/MAPPING hierarchy. Second output includes complex transformation chain (Source Qualifier>Expression>Aggregator>Joiner>Router>Expression>Targets) versus first output's incomplete mapping structure. Only commonality is MEMBERSDATA field definitions.

XML structure syntactically valid but incomplete. Missing closing tags and transformation logic. Source definition properly formatted with all 13 fields correctly defined. DATABASETYPE='Snowflake' and field specifications are accurate. However, mapping lacks target definitions and transformation components required for functional implementation.

Comprehensive and syntactically correct XML structure. Complete PowerMart repository format with proper nesting. All transformation definitions include required fields and expressions. Minor syntax concern: HTML entity encoding in expressions (lines 45-57) using < and > instead of < and > operators, though this is acceptable XML practice. Router conditions and connector definitions are complete and properly structured.
Average of individual agent scores: (70 + 95) / 2 = 82.5, rounded to 82

Complete the mapping implementation by adding target definitions, transformation logic, and proper closing tags. Consider implementing data quality rules similar to second output's comprehensive approach.

Review expression syntax for direct XML compatibility - consider using CDATA sections for complex expressions to avoid HTML entity encoding. Validate all transformation field mappings in development environment.
Standardize on complete PowerMart XML format for consistency. First output should adopt second output's comprehensive structure and DQ implementation approach for production readiness.
